blurb:
This is a solid spot for a casual lunch or a Friday night out when you don't want anything fancy. The kind of place you can bring the whole family without anyone complaining. We've taken family there and everyone found something they liked, which is always a win when you've got a mixed crowd with different tastes.

The food leans on the heartier side, so just know going in that this isn't light fare. Some people in our group felt it ran a little greasy, but nobody pushed their plate away. The service got consistent praise from everyone at the table, which goes a long way when you're wrangling a group.

Friday and Saturday they stay open until 10, which gives you a little more flexibility if the evening runs long.

blurb:
Laredos is the move when you want a sit-down Mexican dinner without driving all the way into the city. It's a colorful cantina with traditional decorations and the kind of vibe that works equally well for a Tuesday night dinner or a Saturday happy hour with friends. My wife and I have landed here more than a few times after not being able to agree on anything else.

The Mexican staples are solid and the margaritas are the real draw for a lot of regulars. If you're going on a weekend, get there a little early or expect a wait. Worth it.

They're open until 10:30 on Fridays and Saturdays, so it's a good option if you want to grab dinner on the later side.
address: 1860 Sandy Plains Rd, Marietta, GA 30066, USA

blurb:
This is where my wife and I end up when we want Thai and don't feel like driving far. It's on Roswell Road, the dining room is spacious with a lot of red in the decor, and the whole vibe is relaxed enough for a weeknight but nice enough for a low-key date night. Over 4.6 stars on nearly a thousand reviews tells you this place has been doing something right for a while.

The Thai noodles and curries are the core of the menu and people keep coming back for them. Classic dishes done well, not overly Americanized. If you like your food with some heat, they'll usually accommodate.

A couple things worth knowing before you go: they're closed on Sundays, and they break between lunch and dinner service on weekdays. Lunch wraps up around 2:30 and dinner doesn't start until 5 or 5:30 depending on the day. Plan around that and you're good.

blurb:
This one is a genuine hidden gem and I mean that. It's a taqueria inside a supermarket on Allgood Road and it does not look like much from the outside. But the food is legit. The kind of place where you can tell someone actually cooked it, not just reheated it.

The pastor is what you need to order. Seriously. People who've been going here rave about how well seasoned it is, and the tortas are outstanding. The pupusas are also worth getting, especially the beans and cheese. The chicken plate with rice and beans has a following too, and reviewers specifically called out that the beans are homemade, not from a can. That detail matters.

Prices are low and they're open every day, which makes this an easy call for a cheap, filling lunch or a quick weeknight dinner.

blurb:
This place has become a regular stop for me whenever I need a break from the usual coffee shop routine. It's got a cheerful, laid-back feel and the kind of energy that makes you want to grab a table and stay a while. Good for a slow weekend morning, a midday coffee run, or whenever you just want something a little different than your standard American bakery.

The Brazilian light bites are the draw here, and the traditional cakes and cookies are worth trying if you haven't had Brazilian pastries before. They do things a little differently than what you're used to, in a good way. My wife is a fan of the sweeter options and there's usually a solid rotating selection to pick from.

They're open pretty late for a bakery, seven days a week, which is rare and genuinely useful when you're craving something in the evening and most spots are already closed up.
address: 1260 Powers Ferry Rd A, Marietta, GA 30067, USA
